Isn't this better than a Google Doc?

Your excuse to touch grass this summer

A centralized tournament package for debaters, judges, and guests.

This page is the final tournament package for Waterloo IV 2026. Scroll through for logistics, deadlines, policies, and resource links for this year's tournament.

Open the packageSee the weekend
Registration:OPEN
Billeting:AVAILABLE
Dinos:WELCOME

Clock tower

Important dates

These are hard deadlines. If you have any questions or concerns, reach out to the TD team!

Deadline

Institutional Registration

Submit team and judge numbers

May 11th

Open link→

Deadline

Independent Team Registration

Teams not competing under an institution use this form. Hybrid teams are welcome, as are debaters no longer enrolled in a university

May 11th

Open link→

Deadline

Participant Information

This includes names, billeting, etc...

May 17th

Deadline

Payment deadline

Payment instructions will be sent after registration is complete. Reach out to the TD team if you have any concerns

May 15th

Pocket portal

Links you need on hand

Quick links stay near the top. Make sure to have a look through all of them.

Open→

Rulebook

Format, eligibility, break, and logistics

Everything you need to know about how the tournament is actually running

Info

Format

British Parliamentary, 5 in rounds, 4 open adjudications

Info

Categories and eligibility

Break to open semifinals and novice finals. For a team to be eligible for novice break, noth debaters must be novice.

Info

Judge feedback

In order for teams to remain break eligible, they must submit judge feedback on Tabbycat for all the OAs they receive.

Info

Motions and prep

Walking time will be allocated for all debaters to get to their rooms prior to motion drop, followed by a 15 minute prep period.

Treasure chest

Fees, fines, and money things

Pay up! If you need a waiver in order to attend, please reach out to the TD team ASAP :)

Info

Team registration fee

$160

Info

Judge registration and missing judge fee

Each judge registration is $50. Institutions who do not meet the N-1 judge rule must pay a fee of $50 per missing judge

Info

Independent adjudicator compensation

When filling out the IA application form, specificy your total desired compensation

Weekend arc

Tournament Schedule

This is a tentative schedule, but things are unlikely to change. Any dietary restrictions should be submitted in the participant information form.

Saturday

May 23rd

8:30 AM - 9:00 AM

Check in and breakfast

Hard drop at 9:00 AM will be enforced

9:00 AM - 9:45 AM

Briefings

Tournament, debater, judge, and equity briefings

9:45 AM - 11:45 AM

Round 1

Open Adjudication

11:45 AM - 12:30 PM

Lunch

12:30 PM - 2:30 PM

Round 2

Open Adjudication

2:30 PM - 4:30 PM

Round 3

Open Adjudication

4:30 PM - 6:30 PM

Round 4

Closed Adjudication

8:30 PM

Social

Firepit on the Lake

Sunday

May 24th

8:45 AM - 9:30 AM

Check In and Breakfast

9:30 AM - 11:30 AM

Round 5

Open Adjudication

11:30 AM - 12:15 PM

Lunch

12:15 PM - 12:30 PM

Break Announcement

12:30 PM - 2:30 PM

Semifinals and Novice Finals

2:30 PM - 4:30 PM

Grand Finals

4:45 PM

Awards

Field guide

Logistics in Waterloo

Travel, parking, food, accessibility, and housing

Address

200 University Ave W, Waterloo

Travel notes

  • •The University of Waterloo is accessible by GO Transit.
  • •There are a number of GO Bus routes that take you directly to the University of Waterloo (routes 25 from Square One, 30 from Bramalea and 17 from Hamilton through Guelph).
  • •The GO Bus stop is on campus and a ~10 min walk from the GA (EV3).
  • •If you are coming in from Toronto Union Station: Take the GO Train (Kitchener Line) directly to Kitchener GO.
  • •Walk to the Central ION Stop (~10 minutes from Kitchener GO) and take the ION North to the University of Waterloo Station.
  • •NOTE: The Kitchener Line does not run all the way to Kitchener GO at all times of day and does not run on weekends.
  • •If necessary, you will need to take the Kitchener GO to/from Bramalea and then take the 30 to/from the University of Waterloo station.

Parking

Parking on campus will be available near the building . Please use university facilities respectfully and follow procedures, including paying any required visitor parking fees.

Billeting

If you are billeting, please check the billeting allocation email for more overnight parking info or contact your host

House rules

Policies and Expectations

Tech, Equity, and Opt-Out policies will be released once confirmed by their organisers

Info

Eligibility

Hybrid teams are allowed to attend! This tournament is not a strict IV, dinos are welcome! Novice Eligibility: Speakers in their first year of competitive university debate, or who have attended less than three tournaments prior to this one will be eligible for novice status.

Info

Notice of Recording & Photography

By attending this event, you consent to being photographed or recorded for promotional purposes/educational purposes and/or personal use of attendees. If you have concerns regarding being recorded/photographed, please contact the TD team (we'd love to have nice photos of you though😊).
